title: Q&A: The Midnight Sun - SKY LIGHTS
content:
### Locations and Geographic Data

| Location | Specific Geographic Detail | Latitude |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Arctic Circle | Boundary for phenomenon | 66°33′46.3″ |
| Fort Yukon, Alaska | Right on the Arctic Circle | Not explicitly stated |
| Barrow, Alaska | Farther north than Fort Yukon | Not explicitly stated |
| Research stations | South of the Antarctic Circle | Not explicitly stated |

### Observation Dates and Durations

| Location | Phenomenon | Start Date | End Date | Duration |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Northern Hemisphere | Midnight Sun (Summer Solstice) | June 21 | June 21 | Day with greatest amount of daylight |
| Northern Hemisphere | Noon Darkness (Winter Solstice) | December 21 | December 21 | 24 hours with no Sun |
| Fort Yukon (North city limits) | Midnight Sun | Not explicitly stated | Not explicitly stated | One day each year |
| Fort Yukon (North city limits) | Noon Darkness | Not explicitly stated | Not explicitly stated | One day each year |
| Barrow, Alaska | Midnight Sun | May 15 | July 28 | Not explicitly stated |
| Barrow, Alaska | Noon Darkness | November 18 | January 16 | Not explicitly stated |

### Movement of the Sun and Celestial Mechanics
*   **Continuous Daylight:** A region north of the Arctic Circle "stays in daylight continuously and never enters into night."
*   **Vertical Movement:** The Sun "dips low in the sky, but never drops below the horizon."
*   **Position at Midnight:** "The Sun is 'up' at midnight."
*   **Earth's Tilt:** Fixed in space at an angle of 23.5° to the plane of its orbit.
*   **Variation:** The tilt with respect to the Sun varies between ±23.5° over the course of one orbit.
*   **Orientation (Summer Solstice):** The northern hemisphere is "tilted toward the Sun."
*   **Orientation (Winter Solstice):** The northern hemisphere is "tilted away from the Sun."

### Observation Technical Details
*   **Animation Software:** Celestia (open source app).
*   **Animation Speed-up Factor:** 7680X.
*   **Visual Indicators:** The terminator (day/night line).
*   **Photo Evidence:** A 5-hour time-lapse centered around midnight.

### Specific Terminology and Names
*   **Midnight Sun:** Defined as when the Sun stays up for 24 hours and is visible at midnight.
*   **Noon Darkness:** 24 hours with no Sun (occurs during Winter Solstice).
*   **Summer Solstice:** June 21 in the northern hemisphere.
*   **Winter Solstice:** Dec 21 in the northern hemisphere.
*   **TW:** Individual from Vancouver, British Columbia who queried the phenomenon.
*   **The Arctic Circle:** Latitude 66°33′46.3″.
